title: panicum capillare
intro: Panicum capillare, also known as little millet or witch grass, is a type of annual grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can help to soothe skin irritations and prevent infections. The health benefits of Panicum capillare include its ability to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems are often used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
